rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report 'named thing must be an expression with a type' or '<error>' types, resolve the root cause by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before adding new architecture.
If a guarantee or assumption references an undefined identifier like 'door1' or 'handle_position', remove the invalid clause rather than attempting to add missing architecture elements.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference features or variables that are explicitly declared or visible in the current component scope.
Use assign statements only for outputs or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scopes.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation-level scopes; keep them in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have valid types; ensure all identifiers resolve to valid AADL features or declared constants.
Binary expressions in AGREE must match operand types; e.g., boolean expressions must compare boolean values.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to repair AGREE diagnostics by introducing new component ports or subcomponents without first resolving the root reference errors.
Do not add new architecture elements when diagnostics indicate unresolved identifiers or invalid types in the same line.
